summary: Iteration 107 Calendar Page testing complete. All backend Calendar API endpoints working correctly (11/11 tests passed). Frontend Calendar page fully functional with Month/Week/List views, event creation, event side panel, and navigation. All navigation paths to /calendar work (sidebar, toolbar icon, mobile bottom nav).
seed_data_creation: Created and deleted test events during testing
context_for_next_testing_agent: Calendar page at /calendar route is fully functional. Backend endpoints: GET /api/calendar/events (derived KOLOR events), GET /api/calendar/google-events (returns connected:false when not connected), POST /api/calendar/events (create manual event), DELETE /api/calendar/events/:id (delete manual event). Frontend has Month/Week/List views with proper data-testid attributes. Navigation to calendar works from: sidebar (data-testid='sidebar-calendar'), toolbar icon (data-testid='view-calendar'), mobile bottom nav (data-testid='bottom-nav-calendar'). Event creation modal opens from Add Event button or clicking a day cell. Event side panel shows when clicking an event with View Lead and Delete Event actions.

backend: GET /api/calendar/events returns derived KOLOR events (from Leads, Quotes, Contracts) plus manual CalendarEvent entries
GET /api/calendar/events works with and without date params (uses defaults)
GET /api/calendar/google-events returns { connected: false, events: [] } when no Google Calendar connected
POST /api/calendar/events creates a manual event and returns it correctly
POST /api/calendar/events supports timed events with startTime/endTime
All calendar endpoints require authentication (return 401 without auth)
frontend: Calendar page loads at /calendar route with data-testid='calendar-page'
Month view displays by default with data-testid='calendar-month-view'
Week view toggle works with data-testid='calendar-view-week'
List view toggle works with data-testid='calendar-view-list'
Today button returns to current date
Add Event button opens Create Event modal
Clicking a day cell opens Create Event modal with date pre-filled
Create Event modal has title input, date input, all-day toggle, time fields, notes, save/cancel buttons
Creating an event via modal saves successfully and shows on calendar
Event side panel opens when clicking an event
Event side panel shows event details (title, date, time, notes)
Event side panel has Delete Event button for manual events
Delete Event removes the event and shows success toast
Dashboard sidebar Calendar link navigates to /calendar
Dashboard toolbar calendar icon navigates to /calendar
Mobile bottom nav Calendar button visible and navigates to /calendar
Back to Dashboard button works from Calendar page
